meaning: n.姘旇川,閬撲箟,姘戞棌绮剧,绀句細鎬濇疆,椋庢皵
examples: Critics say the digital access codes represent the same profit-seeking ethos of the textbook business, and are even harder for students to opt out of.
The whole ethos of the hotel is effortless service.
Dr. Ferlic proposed a surgical intervention that reflects precisely the high-tech ethos of contemporary American medicine.
But this really has little to do with the essential ethos of American agriculture.
